channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Vancouver Officials Outline Preparations for FIFA World Cup 2026
item.description: With the FIFA World Cup 2026 set to kick off in just over a month, Vancouver officials, alongside FIFA's host committee and regional transport authorities, provided an update on the city's readiness f...

item.title: DPWH projects full control of Navotas landfill fire within a week
item.description: The Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) announced that the ongoing fire at the Navotas sanitary landfill is expected to be fully extinguished by May 11, after nearly a month of firefighting...

item.title: Canadian Journalist Raises Funds for Ukrainian Orphans Through Sticker Sales
item.description: Canadian reporter and filmmaker Christian Borys launched a fundraising initiative by selling stickers to support organizations helping children affected by the war in Ukraine. Inspired by a meme calle...

item.title: US residents flood Canadian archives after new citizenship rules open door to 'Lost Canadians' descendants
item.description: Archivists and genealogists across Canada have reported an unprecedented surge in requests from Americans seeking to prove Canadian ancestry since the enactment of Bill C‑3, known as the “Lost Canadia...

item.title: Commission hears procedural failures before 541kg cocaine stolen from Hawks facility
item.description: During proceedings at the Madlanga commission of inquiry, SAPS Major-General Hendrik Flynn highlighted significant lapses in the handling and documentation of a 541kg cocaine seizure later stolen from...

item.title: The Conversation Africa: 11 Years of Sharing African Expertise and Influencing Policy
item.description: Over de last 11 years, The Conversation Africa has published nearly 13,000 articles by over 8,000 authors, giving public, national, and global policymakers access to African and worldwide academic exp...

item.title: Israeli Court Denies Appeal of Flotilla Activists Against Detention
item.description: An Israeli court has officially rejected the appeal filed by activists involved in the Global Sumud Flotilla, who were challenging their detention. The flotilla activists, including individuals such a...

item.title: Analysis of electoral promises and state finances in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu
item.description: The article discusses the comparison between the promises made by political parties during elections and the actual trends in public finances in West Bengal and Tamil Nadu. It explores whether the com...

item.title: Reflections on Making Moulin Rouge! 25 Years On: Cast and Crew Memories
item.description: Moulin Rouge!, directed by Baz Luhrmann and released in 2001, marked a bold revitalisation of the musical genre. The film recounts the doomed romance between Satine, a cabaret star played by Nicole Ki...

item.title: TG Jones to close 150 former WH Smith branches, putting hundreds of jobs at risk
item.description: The high-street arm of WH Smith, recently acquired and rebranded as TG Jones by private equity firm Modella Capital, is set to close 150 of its 480 stores. The closures, announced as part of a broader...

item.title: Documenting the Work of Ukrainian Artillery Through Photography
item.description: Arsenii Gerasymenko, a Kyiv-based photographer, shifted from fashion photography to documenting the realities of war on the Ukrainian front. Since 2022, he has been capturing the lives and operations ...

item.title: Historian Zbigniew Gluza Discusses Challenges and Opportunities in Ukrainian-Polish Relations
item.description: Historian Zbigniew Gluza, a prominent Polish expert on Eastern European history, offers a nuanced perspective on Ukrainian-Polish relations, examining historical tensions and the possibilities for rec...

item.title: Small business owner warns rate hikes and inflation may force further closures
item.description: A Sydney bakery owner has warned that ongoing interest rate hikes and rising living costs are putting small businesses under severe pressure, following the closure of one of her stores. Sina Klug, who...

item.title: Kenyan Woman Ends Friendship after Bride Asks for Money for Wedding Meal
item.description: A Kenyan woman shared a surprising WhatsApp conversation with a friend who recently got married, causing a stir online. According to the leaked chats, the bride contacted her guest, Moinani, saying th...

item.title: Zanzibar introduces digital platforms to improve tourism revenue collection and pension delivery
item.description: Zanzibar has taken a major step in modernising its public services by launching new digital systems aimed at improving tourism revenue collection and ensuring timely pension payments for the elderly. ...

item.title: Saskatchewan Passes Law Allowing Involuntary Addiction Treatment Amid Concerns
item.description: The Saskatchewan government has passed the Compassionate Intervention Act, which will allow individuals with severe addictions to be placed into treatment involuntarily. As early as this fall, people ...

item.title: Naveed Akram hit with 19 additional charges over 2025 Bondi shooting
item.description: Naveed Akram, the man accused of the 2025 Bondi Beach shooting, has been slapped with 19 new charges while being held in Goulburn's supermax prison. These fresh charges include ten counts of shooting ...

item.title: Teacher and partner on trial for abuse and death of adopted infant
item.description: Jamie Varley, 37, a former secondary school teacher and head of year, and his partner, John McGowan-Fazakerley, 32, are facing trial at Preston Crown Court for the abuse and subsequent death of 13-mon...

item.title: Evaton Residents Struggle as Multimillion-Rand Ablution Project Remains Inaccessible
item.description: In Evaton, Gauteng, over 20 families are forced to rely on just two communal toilets they built themselves, despite the government spending over R24 million on 30 new ablution facilities. The provinci...

item.title: Court told suspect in arson attacks linked to Keir Starmer claimed no knowledge of Prime Minister
item.description: A court in London has heard that a Ukrainian man accused of carrying out arson attacks on properties linked to Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer claimed he did not know who the Prime Minister was. Roman...
